I'm lost here:-s
my specs:

### Post by saru411 on 2007-09-19
you must open nvidia-settings as root. that means try this in terminal

> sudo nvidia-settings

this will open nvidia settings with root privileges. you must have root to save xorg files.

once it is open chage what you wanted to change and save it. restart x (ctrl+alt+backspace) and you should load the correct settings that you set previously.

hope that helps

---

### Post by dabl on 2007-09-19
In a console, try ```
sudo nvidia-xconfig --add-argb-glx-visuals --composite
```

When it is done writing your xorg.conf, restart the xserver with Ctrl-Alt-Backspace.

Now run ```
sudo nvidia-settings
```, and the only thing you should mess with is your screen resolution.  You can have it "detect display" if you want. Then click the "Save to X Configuration File" button, and the only thing it will change is the default screen resolution.

That's it -- I hope this helps!  :guitar:

---
